name: WorldView (Maxar legacy)

overview.value: Vantor's satellite constellation page lists WorldView Legion, WorldView-3, WorldView-2, GeoEye-1 and WorldView-1 as part of its imaging satellite fleet. Vantor describes its constellation of 10 imaging satellites as able to revisit the same location on Earth 15 times per day, with downlink speeds as fast as 15 minutes after collection.

base.reason: SpaceNews (trade press), reporting Vantor's April 8, 2026 statement: 'Vantor's existing fleet, composed of six WorldView Legion satellites and four legacy spacecraft'; corroborated by Vantor's own constellation page naming exactly WorldView-1, WorldView-2, WorldView-3 and GeoEye-1 as non-Legion fleet members, and by this profile's sats_active_verified (CelesTrak, value 4)

eoPortal mission page (ESA-run aggregator); page states WorldView-3 achieves '31 cm in the panchromatic band at nadir'; this is WorldView-3's own figure, the highest-resolution member of the legacy fleet grouped in this profile, not a fleet-wide statement covering WorldView-1/2 or GeoEye-1 (registry v2 EO spec backfill, bonus task, cite-facts-only per terms)
eoPortal mission page; page states 'The satellite covers a ground swath of 13.1km'; WorldView-3 specific figure

revisit.value: WorldView-3: under one day for any location at 1 m resolution or better; about 4.5 days at 20 degrees off-nadir or less

base.reason: eoPortal mission page; page states 'WorldView-3 delivers a revisit time of under one day for any given location on Earth with a 1 m ground resolution or better. Revisit times for an off-nadir angle of 20º or less is on the order of 4.5 days.'

eoPortal mission page; page states the instrument carries '29 total bands: 1 panchromatic (450-800 nm), 8 multispectral VNIR bands (400-1040 nm range), 8 SWIR bands (1195-2365 nm range), and 12 CAVIS bands (405-2245 nm range)'; WorldView-3 specific

notes: Maxar's legacy WorldView satellites; WorldView Legion has its own profile. Maxar Intelligence rebranded to Vantor. Seeded for the Orbits surface on Florian's instruction (2026-07-05); sats_active_claimed filled 2026-07-09. sats_launched_total and status stay null: no single page states a combined cumulative-launched figure or fleet-wide status word for this WorldView-plus-GeoEye-1 grouping (WorldView-4 retired 2019, reentered 2021, per Wikipedia/Space.com) without summing across sources, which this site's rules disallow. EO spec fields (2026-07-13, bonus task) are sourced from eoPortal's WorldView-3 mission page specifically, the sharpest and most capable member of this legacy grouping; WorldView-1, WorldView-2 and GeoEye-1 carry different (lower) resolutions not stated on this page, so these figures describe WorldView-3 rather than the fleet as a whole. Page last updated Jul 31, 2023.

events.headline: DigitalGlobe launches WorldView-1, first satellite in the WorldView constellation
DigitalGlobe launches WorldView-4 sub-half-meter imaging satellite
WorldView-4 loses pointing control after gyroscope failure
